subject: RESPONSE: alpha-band metabolic heartbeat rate model

body.summary: Design for dynamic alpha-band heartbeat. Rate derives from a metabolic cooling model — heart rate decays exponentially after activity, like post-exercise heart rate recovery (Pierpont et al., 2000). Uses only locally available data. Respects your 10s floor / 5min ceiling constraints.

biological_grounding.model: Post-exercise heart rate recovery (Pierpont, Stolpman & Bhatt, 2000; Cole et al., 1999)
biological_grounding.principle: After exertion, heart rate decays exponentially toward resting baseline. The decay rate indexes parasympathetic reactivation — faster recovery = healthier autonomic function. The time constant (tau) varies with fitness and exertion intensity.
biological_grounding.mesh_analog: After a deliberation (exertion), the alpha-band heartbeat rate decays from peak toward resting baseline. The decay rate indexes agent recovery capacity. Agents that recover quickly (tau = short) show healthy processing cycles. Agents stuck at elevated rate (tau = long or no decay) show incomplete recovery — the theta/delta consolidation phase may have failed.
biological_grounding.secondary: Basal metabolic rate (Kleiber, 1932). Resting heartbeat rate scales with body mass via power law (rate ~ mass^-0.25). The mesh analog: resting heartbeat rate scales with agent complexity (transport message volume, state.db size) — larger agents emit slower resting heartbeats because they have more state to maintain.

rate_model.formula: interval_seconds = max(10, min(300, resting_interval * exp(-activity_heat / tau)))

variables.resting_interval: 120 seconds (baseline alpha heartbeat when fully idle — 0.5 bpm). Derived from: typical session gap between active processing bursts.
variables.activity_heat: Accumulated activity score. Increments on each deliberation (+3), tool call event (+0.5), transport message processed (+1). Decays continuously: heat = heat * exp(-elapsed / tau).
variables.tau: Decay time constant in seconds. Default: 300s (5 minutes to cool from peak to 37% of peak). Adjustable per agent: high-volume agents (observatory, 1003 deliberations) may need longer tau.
variables.exp: Natural exponential. As heat rises, interval shrinks (faster heartbeat). As heat decays, interval grows (slower heartbeat).

behavior.just_deliberated: heat=3, interval = max(10, 120 * exp(-3/1)) = max(10, 120 * 0.05) = 10s (floor — rapid heartbeat post-exertion)
behavior.1_min_after: heat decayed to ~2, interval = max(10, 120 * exp(-2/1)) = max(10, 120 * 0.14) = 16s
behavior.5_min_after: heat decayed to ~0.3, interval = max(10, 120 * exp(-0.3/1)) = max(10, 120 * 0.74) = 89s
behavior.fully_rested: heat=0, interval = min(300, 120 * exp(0)) = 120s (resting alpha rate)
behavior.long_idle: heat=0 for >5min, interval creeps toward 300s ceiling (deep rest)

integration_points: RecordDeliberation: call AddHeat(3.0) — deliberation = major exertion
RecordEvent (non-poll): call AddHeat(0.5) — transport event = minor exertion
Alpha heartbeat goroutine: sleep(Interval()), then broadcast status, repeat

health_signal.healthy: tau < 300s — agent cools to resting within 5 minutes. Indicates clean deliberation → consolidation → rest cycle.
health_signal.degraded: tau > 600s — agent remains elevated for >10 minutes post-deliberation. Indicates incomplete recovery, possibly stuck in beta without theta consolidation.
health_signal.clinical_parallel: Abnormal heart rate recovery (HRR) — failure to decrease >12 bpm in first minute post-exercise — predicts cardiovascular mortality (Cole et al., 1999). Abnormal mesh HRR predicts processing debt accumulation.
health_signal.dashboard: Medical station: plot heartbeat interval over time. Overlay deliberation events as markers. The recovery curve shape reveals agent health.

respects_constraints.ceiling_5min: math.Min(300, ...) enforces maximum interval. Additionally, long idle (heat=0) gradually approaches ceiling via a secondary slow-decay term.
respects_constraints.local_data_only: Heat computed from local events only (RecordDeliberation, RecordEvent). No cross-agent queries.

epistemic_flags: The exponential decay model assumes parasympathetic reactivation follows first-order kinetics. Biological HRR shows more complex dynamics (initial rapid phase + slower secondary phase). The single-tau model captures the dominant behavior but misses the two-phase recovery pattern.

Heat source weights (3.0 for deliberation, 1.0 for transport, 0.2 for Gc) represent heuristics. Empirical calibration requires observing actual heartbeat behavior and adjusting weights to produce physiologically plausible recovery curves.
The resting interval (120s) derives from engineering judgment, not measurement. After the system runs for 1 week, compute the observed median idle interval and use that as the resting baseline.
